title: Summary suit procedure - faster recovery for liquidated claims
p1: Most people assume filing a civil case always means years of waiting in court. But for certain debts, the law offers a surprisingly fast-track route that many professionals don’t even know exists.
p2: The common misconception is that every money dispute must go through a full trial. In reality, when the claim is 'liquidated' (a fixed, undisputed amount like unpaid invoices or written contracts), courts can use a Summary Suit procedure.
p3: Summary Suit procedure is a fast-track mechanism under civil law for liquidated claims—fixed, clearly documented amounts like unpaid invoices or written loan agreements. The defendant cannot automatically file a full defence; they must first seek the court’s permission, and it is granted only if a real defence exists. This filters out delay tactics and speeds up recovery. For businesses, the takeaway is simple: clear contracts, invoices, and written acknowledgements can turn a long dispute into a much quicker resolution path.
conclusion: Have you ever dealt with a payment dispute where documentation made all the difference in how quickly things were resolved?
